reason: Modifying the retry parameter in /etc/security/pwquality.conf only changes the number of password retry prompts allowed per session. This is a standard password policy hardening step that does not affect authentication mechanisms, SSH access, or critical services, and poses no risk of lockout.
estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; Setting retry too low may cause user frustration due to frequent prompts, but does not cause system lockout or service disruption.
